title: Boat Rental in St Petersburg: Costs and What to Know 2026
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in St Petersburg starts at the Vinoy Basin and the Municipal Marina, where shared bay cruises begin near EUR 40 per seat and small motorboats run from about EUR 280 for a half day. BoatHire24 lists skippered and bareboat options across Tampa Bay for most budgets.

Where to set off in St Petersburg

St Petersburg sits on the Pinellas peninsula, with sheltered launch points on both the Tampa Bay side and the Gulf side. Most charters leave from a handful of well known marinas, each suiting a different kind of day.

  • St Petersburg Municipal Marina: the central downtown base, minutes from the Pier District and open Tampa Bay.
  • Vinoy Basin: a calm anchorage by the historic Vinoy district, ideal for boarding before a bay cruise.
  • Maximo Marina: near the southern tip, handy for runs out towards the Skyway and the Gulf passes.
  • Gulfport Municipal Marina: a quieter launch on Boca Ciega Bay just west of the city.
  • Tierra Verde: a marina village close to Fort De Soto Park and the islands at the bay mouth.

Which boat to rent in St Petersburg?

The best boat depends on group size, whether you want speed or shade, and how far towards the Gulf you plan to roam. Day trips here range from nimble centre consoles for sandbar hopping to crewed motor yachts for sunset cruising.

  1. Centre console: best for fishing and sandbar days, seats 4 to 6 guests, from EUR 320 for a half day.
  2. Pontoon boat: best for relaxed family outings on the bay, seats up to 10 guests, from EUR 350 per day.
  3. Bowrider: best for swimming and short Gulf runs, seats 6 to 8 guests, from EUR 380 per day.
  4. Motor yacht: best for comfort and sunset cruising, seats 8 to 12 guests, from EUR 1,100 per day with a skipper, and you can compare this motor yacht for a fuller idea of the layout.
  5. Catamaran: best for stable, shaded group days, seats up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,400 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in St Petersburg?

Expect roughly EUR 40 to EUR 2,500 per day, depending on whether you join a shared tour or charter a private crewed yacht. Prices below are typical 2026 starting points, and fuel, gratuity and any captain fee are sometimes added separately. If you would rather avoid surprises, you can find a skippered charter with fixed all-in pricing for the whole day.

  • Shared bay or dolphin tour: from about EUR 40 per person for a 90 minute trip.
  • Small motorboat half day: from about EUR 280 for roughly four hours.
  • Catamaran full day: from about EUR 1,400 for up to twelve guests.
  • Crewed motor yacht: from about EUR 1,100, rising towards EUR 2,500 for larger vessels.

What season is best for boat rental in St Petersburg?

The prime boating window runs roughly November to April, when humidity drops and the afternoon storms of summer ease off. Sea breezes build through the day, and the local sea breeze front can bring quick squalls inland on hot afternoons.

For the calmest water, launch from the Vinoy Basin early and aim to reach Shell Key Preserve before the afternoon sea breeze picks up around midday; the morning glass-off makes the short hop across the bay noticeably smoother.

St Petersburg lies in the United States, where boating rules are set largely at state level, and Florida ties operator requirements to a boater education card rather than a traditional licence for many visitors. The operator confirms the exact requirements for your boat and date, so always check before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 40 to EUR 2,500 per day.
  • Peak months: November to April.
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours.
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests on most day boats.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Nearest island run: under 5 nautical miles to Shell Key Preserve.

Questions about boat rental in St Petersburg

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in St Petersburg?

In Florida many renters complete a short boater education requirement rather than hold a full licence, and crewed charters remove the issue entirely. The operator will confirm what your specific boat and date require before departure.

Can I see dolphins on a St Petersburg boat trip?

Bottlenose dolphins are common in Tampa Bay and along the Gulf passes, and many shared tours focus on spotting them. Sightings are never guaranteed, but morning trips towards the bay mouth tend to offer the best chances.

How far is it to the open Gulf from St Petersburg?

From the Municipal Marina you can reach the passes near Fort De Soto and the open Gulf in well under an hour at cruising speed. The exact run depends on your boat, the wind and how many stops you make.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in St Petersburg or list your own, and it brings local operators together for 2026 trips across Tampa Bay and the Gulf passes. To compare dates and crews, browse the listings and request the day that suits your group.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in St Petersburg and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
